name: National Botanic Garden of Wales
terrain: Gravel and tarmac paths, some grass meadow
highlights: World's largest single-span glasshouse set in Regency lakes, meadows and Welsh rainforest.
points_of_interest: Norman Foster's Great Glasshouse; Butterfly House; Regency lakes; Welsh Apothecary
viewpoints: Rock garden viewpoint; Paxton's Tower in distance
parking_start: National Botanic Garden car park (entry fee)
food_drink_nearby: NBGW Seasons Restaurant; Y Plas Café
hazards_notes: Entry fee; no dogs; closes late winter afternoons
